---
title: "rig run"
---

<!-- Generated by website/gen-cli-reference.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

## `rig run`

```text
Description:
  Run R, an R script or an R project, using the selected R version.

  All of these examples allow an `--r-version` argument, to use a specific
  R version.

  Start R:
    rig run
  Run an R script:
    rig run -f <script-file>
  Evaluate an R expression:
    rig run -e <expression>
  Run a script from a package (from its `exec` directory):
    rig run <pkg>::<script>
  Run an R app:
    rig run <path-to-app>

  Currently supported apps are:
  - Plumber APIs,
  - Shiny apps,
  - Quarto documents embedding Shiny apps,
  - Quarto documents,
  - Rmd documents,
  - Rmd documents embedding Shiny apps,
  - Static web sites.

Usage: rig run [OPTIONS] [command]...

Arguments:
  [command]...
          R script or project to run, with parameters

Options:
  -r, --r-version <r-version>
          R version to use

  -t, --app-type <app-type>
          Explicitly specify app type to run
          
          [possible values: api, shiny, quarto-shiny, rmd-shiny, quarto-static, rmd-static, static]

      --dry-run
          Show the command, but do not run it

      --startup
          Print R startup message

      --no-startup
          Do not print R startup message

      --echo
          Print input to R

      --no-echo
          Do not print input to R

  -e, --eval <eval>
          R expression to evaluate

      --user
          Run in user mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)

      --admin
          Run in admin mode (overrides RIG_MODE and config)

  -f, --script <script>
          R script file to run

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')
```
